entry.title: Process Manager Simulation
entry.content:
I've created a simple process flow to investigate an issue.

Volume coming in

One task (Warehouse Picking)

Volume coming out

Due to the volume coming in, the number of resources available and the cycle time of the task (this is set to 100% constant), the process should complete successfully and all picks completed.

What i'm finding is that the task still has a number of items still to be picked at the end of the simulation? I don't understand why?

This is to baseline a broader process moving forward, and in reality i know the operation can actually get through the work. So why is the simulation not showing that? Are wait times being factored in for some reason? And if so, can these be removed somewhere?

Help appreciated......

entry.title: ¿Eres estudiante de carrera afín a la tecnología y te interesa el mundo SAP? ¡Esto es para ti!
entry.content:
¡Oportunidad única para estudiantes de tecnología – Evento organizado por ASUGMEX!

¿Estás en el último semestre de tu carrera o recién egresado/a de una disciplina relacionada con la tecnología? ¿Te interesa conocer más sobre el mundo SAP? ¡Entonces esta sesión es para ti!

Fecha: 29 de mayo
Hora: 13:30 – 14:30 (horario de México)

Sobre la sesión: 

La tecnología mueve al mundo, y SAP está al frente de esta transformación. Si estás en el último semestre de tu carrera o recién egresado/a de una disciplina afín a la tecnología, queremos invitarte a esta sesión especial donde descubrirás cómo SAP lidera la innovación y cómo ASUGMEX puede ayudarte a formar parte de este ecosistema.

En esta sesión te explicaremos en detalle nuestra membresía estudiantil gratuita, una oportunidad única para acceder a sesiones virtuales sobre funcionalidad, procesos, casos de uso, innovación, integraciones, tecnología y mucho más. También conocerás los diferentes caminos para capacitarte y definir un plan de carrera en el mundo de SAP.

Al formar parte de esta membresía, tendrás acceso a una Asociación que reúne a empresas usuarias de SAP, implementadores, consultores y expertos, lo que te permitirá conectar con líderes de la industria y ampliar tus oportunidades.

Acompáñanos en esta sesión especial para descubrir cómo potenciar tu futuro en SAP.

Temario

1. ¿Qué es ASUG México?
2. Qué incluye y cómo puedes ser parte de la membresía estudiantil
3. Q&A

Oradores(as):

Malena Gutiérrez
Directora General de ASUG México

Más información: ¿Eres estudiante de carrera afín a la tecnología y te interesa el mundo SAP? ¡Esto es para ti! – ASUGMEX

entry.title: Visibility of texts in output management
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

i am creating a sales quotation and entering internal and external texts in the header, for example, Internal Note (TX01) and Sales Note for Customer (TX02). Both texts are being printed in the output preview of the document.
I assumed that internal texts (TX01) would not be printed on the document.

Where can I configure the visibility of texts in document printing within output management?

Thanks in adavance!

Katharina

entry.title: Variant configuration (LO-VC). Charcateristic restriction based on a MultiValue charctaristic values
entry.content:
Hello SAP gurus 🙂

I have a question about variant configuration in SAP LO-VC. Is it possible to restrict a characteristic to only the values that have been entered in a multivalue characteristic within the same configuration?

Example:

  • Color (multivalue): "Red", "Blue", "Black"
  • FurnitureColor (single value): I should only be able to choose one value from these three since they are the selected values for the "Color" characteristic.

I would like to know how to implement this in SAP LO-VC in this dynamic way. Does anyone have experience with this type of configuration and could share an example or guide on how to achieve it?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: BW: Delete content from Write Optimized DSO in Chain
entry.content:
According the error message there looks to be some sort of SQL error. Check the joblog, syslog (sm21) and the corresponding work process trace file from SM50 to see if there are any errors.

If you find nothing, then check in the debugger from where the error/sql exception is coming from.

entry.title: Validation Rule - Compare Opening balances to Prior year balances
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
Is there a way to write validation rules to compare prior year (BS balances) to the BS opening balance (subtype)?
The period mode selections don’t have a prior year YTD option.
We are using 2023 OP FPS01.
I see that the 2502 Cloud version has the updated validation approach where the option to select Closing balance for the previous year is available. ->
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/ef6cd7b35ce94fbf89d4c979d5bf5676.html?q=validation+rule+groups

What can be done with 2023OP?
One option is to copy the prior year ending balance to current year version B and then compare actual to version B but version is not a characteristic available for selection.
Any ideas?

Regards,
Eyal

entry.title: SAP Fiori - Remove Adapt Filter Button
entry.content:
Hello. Im trying to create a list report page, and  I want to remove a "Adapt Filters" button.

This is what my page looks like

I did restrict what fields are available to filter, so in my "Adapt Filters" only appears this ones


But still, I do not want the adapt filter to be shown.

This is my annotations.cds file content:

using CENCERROService as service from '../../srv/service';

/* COLUMNS */
annotate service.ORDENTRABAJO with @(
    UI.LineItem : [
        { $Type : 'UI.DataField', Label : 'Id', Value : Id },
        { $Type : 'UI.DataField', Label : 'Descripcion', Value : Descripcion },
        { $Type : 'UI.DataField', Label : 'Labor', Value : Labor },
        { $Type : 'UI.DataField', Value : Estado, Label : 'Estado' }
    ]
);

/* FILTERS */
annotate service.ORDENTRABAJO with @(
    UI.SelectionFields : [ CampoId, Descripcion,  EstadoId ]
);

/* FILTER: CAMPO */
annotate service.ORDENTRABAJO with {
    CampoId @(
        Common.ValueList : {
            $Type : 'Common.ValueListType',
            CollectionPath : 'CAMPO',
            Parameters : [ { $Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut', LocalDataProperty : CampoId, ValueListProperty : 'Id', }, ],
            Label : 'Campo',
        },
        Common.ValueListWithFixedValues : true,
        Common.Label : 'Campo'
    )
};
annotate service.CAMPO with {
    Id @Common.Text : { $value : Descripcion, ![@UI.TextArrangement] : #TextFirst, }
};

/* FILTER: ESTADO */
annotate service.ORDENTRABAJO with {
    EstadoId @(
        Common.ValueList : {
            $Type : 'Common.ValueListType',
            CollectionPath : 'ESTADO',
            Parameters : [ { $Type : 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut', LocalDataProperty : EstadoId, ValueListProperty : 'Id', }, ],
            Label : 'Estado',
        },
        Common.ValueListWithFixedValues : true,
        Common.Label : 'Estado'
    )
};
annotate service.ESTADO with {
    Id @Common.Text : { $value : Descripcion, ![@UI.TextArrangement] : #TextFirst, }
};

/* FILTER: DELETE SEARCH INPUT */
annotate service.ORDENTRABAJO with @Capabilities : { 
    SearchRestrictions : {
        $Type : 'Capabilities.SearchRestrictionsType',
        Searchable: false
    },
    FilterRestrictions : {
        $Type : 'Capabilities.FilterRestrictionsType',
        FilterExpressionRestrictions : [
            { Property: 'CampoId', AllowedExpressions: 'SingleValue' }
        ],
        RequiredProperties: [ CampoId ],
        NonFilterableProperties : [ Campo, Estado, HasPlanificadas, HasReales, Id, Labor, LaborId ]
    },
    Deletable : false
};

Any help out there?

entry.title: Re: Incoming IDOCS get stuck in status 64

entry.title: How to specify the usnam data of the rbkp table when creating a MIRO invoice.
entry.content:
When I use the BAPI_INCOMINGINVOICE_PARK BAPI to create a MIRO invoice, I tracked the standard code and found that an asynchronous update process is triggered when inserting data into the rbkp table. The method called by the asynchronous update process is MRM_INVOICE_HEADER_SAVE, within which usnam is directly set to sy-uname. I want to ask how to set it to a user of my choice.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to specify the usnam data of the rbkp table when...
entry.content:
As you have detected in the code, there is a fixed logic that sets SY-UNAME. If there were a way to customize it, there would be places in the same code allowing other values.

From software perspective, there is no reason to include a different user name for the invoice creator.

entry.title: Understanding Crystal Report Connections
entry.content:
This question is regarding the My Connections listed in the Database Expert.

In other development environments, a connection is only the details required to connect to a particular database server and database. You can then run as many different queries as needed that reference the single connection (data source).

It seems that in Crystal Reports a connection is the combination of the data source details AND a single query (Command). Is that correct?

entry.title: Re: RISE with SAP Migration Options, Source ERP on HANA
entry.content: Yes, that's it but only if the original source DB is SAP HANA. SAP Public Cloud only supports HANA databases for ECC.

entry.title: Re: Procurement of Services in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
'Add Contract/Catalog Item' during Service Sheet Creation

If a Supplier does not get the option to 'Add Contract/Catalog Item' while creating a Service Entry Sheet, please check for Default Transaction Rule 'Allow suppliers to access your Ariba procurement applications for collaborative invoicing, requisitioning, managing time and expense sheets, and creating service sheets'.
Also, may need to work with Ariba Team for enabling 'SSP Buyer entitlement' on Buyer Network Admin side. More details in internal Support Note KB0405344.

entry.title: WANT TOLERANCE IN ALTERNATE UNIT OF MEASURE
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

Our product is steel based Tubes and we have base UoM is Kg and but we are selling the products in Kgs as well as Pcs.

we are maintaining the alternate Uom Pc with Kg, But in our steel industry, always conversion factor is not fix with reference to kg due to material thickness characteristics. 

Suppose we have received tubes order from customer in which customer is demanding 1.20mm thickness and but always it is not necessary that production will always be in 1.20mm thickness, it may be vary according to allowable thickness and due to this, conversion factor will not be same. 

our concern is that whenever we make gr against production order into system, system should allow us to change the weight as per actual which will be in defined tolerance limit. currently system is not allowing to change "Qty in SKU", it"s getting auto calculated based on conversion factor maintained in material master.

we are requesting you to suggest us the way, so that our stock can be valued properly.

thanks

entry.title: Re: physical data model using excel import is failing in...
entry.content:
Hi, I tried it with PDM and it seems like a bug to me. George is right about Reference Joins. You have to import them as separate object (on separate excel sheet), but the import still doesn`t work properly. I am still getting "Error: Could not find parent object location with Name = Order.CustomerID". I tried many things (playing with Reference settings in Model Options, changing the order of excel sheets to import, modifying actual values to import, importing with/without ParentKey property on the Relationship,...) nothing helped. But I agree, that standard Excel Import should be capable of that.

You have several options now:

  • debug and repair the Excel Import extension by yourself (thousands of lines of code, uncertain results)
  • write your own import routine (quite easy if you know what you are doing, I`ve done it many times, I can help you with that)
  • do manual repairs after each import

Which option is best for you depends on number of objects to be imported, whether it is one-time or repeated action, how quickly you need it, etc.

HTH and in case you need my help, let me know here (or on LinkedIn),

Ondrej Divis

entry.title: Re: Testing of Developer Extensibility in S4hana Public C...
entry.content:
Hi,

CDS views created in ADT will not be visible in the “Custom CDS Views” APP. But you can see them in the “View Browser” APP. Objects created in ADT can be released for Key User Extensibility and can be used in some Key User APPs.

Best Regards,
Zhehui

entry.title: Re: Stop Reservations Without Stock Available
entry.content:
I'm sorry - but no - you need to stick with standard biz processes. It's perfectly normal to create reservations for materials which you do not have in stock. Otherwise, how would you know what, when to order? How would the MRP run? You should not stop creating reservations just because you don't have stock. 

Please provide more context because right now this requirement seems like a very bad idea.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Elements vs. Freestyle Apps: When to Use What
entry.content:
Hi - It is always great to see SAP Fiori elements in on top of mind for our community. While the summary is catching the main points for SAP Fiori element V2...

... in SAP Fiori elements V4, there are options for developer efficiency AND flexibility

  • Using floorplans is the way to go for maximum developer efficiency. When only tiny things are missing, then the extensions can be used.
  • The flexible programming model as part of SAP Fiori elements V4 allows you to build your custom pages with building blocks, staying within the framework but having the flexibility to use the building blocks and options within the framework. For more information, see: Flexible Programming Model Explorer

Best regards,
Katrin on behalf of the SAP Fiori elements team

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Juhi Kulshreshtha
entry.content:
My full name is Juhi Kulshreshtha and I prefer being called ‘Juhi’. I’m an SAP BTP Enthusiast and working as SAP BTP Competency Lead. My responsibilities include helping the clients stay ahead of the competition by implementing the latest tools and services provided by SAP BTP. I also have keen interest in applications of AI for business scenarios to enhance end-user experience and bring tangible value for the clients.

It is an honour to be nominated by my friend and SAP Community Advocate @StephanieMarley  for the ‘Blog-It-Forward’ series. Though I have written a number of blogs on various SAP topics and events. This is the second time I’m posting on the SAP Community site. My first blog on SAP Community site was about an SIT Event in Bengaluru in India in the year 2022. 

My SAP Journey

I have donned multiple hats in the past and played roles of SAP Project Manager, Architect as well as SME in various areas of SAP. I started my career as an SAP Developer in the areas of ABAP and Webdynpro for Java in the year 2005. 

I have come a long way since then implementing various end-to-end SAP solutions spanning multiple industries and line-of-businesses. 

My areas of expertise in SAP include solutioning in BTP, S/4 HANA and HANA DB Upgrade and ABAP Remediation. My other implementation experiences include SAP Ariba , SAP Concur and SAP FSCD.

My industry experience comprises  - Banking, Insurance, Retail, and Consumer Goods and Services. And my line-of-business experience comprises Finance, Procurement and Supply Chain. I love exploring the newest technologies with a Human-Centered approach.

 

SAP Community Engagements

Being a member of SAP Community provides the opportunity to connect with other SAP Professionals across the SAP world and get the ability to understand different perspectives. Various SAP Events provide a platform to network and gain valuable insights from the experience of others. These events have something for each of the participants irrespective of their experience or areas of expertise in SAP. 

 My engagement with SAP Community has been highest during the last two years, especially when it comes to personally being there as a speaker or an attendee. The latest event that I attended was SAP Inside Track 2025 in Bengaluru . Here are a few pictures with community members -

 

Also, I was fortunate to get the opportunity to speak at SAP TechED 2023 on the topic ‘Exploring Machine Learning with SAP AI Core: Uncovering the What, Why, and How.’ Here are a few photos from the SAP Teched 2023 session -

This session focussed on how organisations can implement ‘Narrow AI’ use cases for their specific requirements using SAP AI Core in BTP. Now BTP has all the capabilities to implement ‘GenAI’ use cases as well. SAP BTP GenAI Hub and Vector DB form important components of such use cases. Not only this, now Joule Studio helps to implement Agentic AI use cases as well. Future roadmap includes customised AI use cases to provide maximum benefit specific to the business scenarios.

SAP in India

SAP India is a well-known brand in the ERP area. It is also recognised as the fastest growing market for SAP SE. It has been recognised as the Top Employer 2025 for the 7th consecutive year. And it is among the top 3 organisations across all industries in India. 

Being a resident of Bengaluru in India, the general sentiment about SAP India is extremely good. And people have high opinions about working in SAP India.

Language in Technology and Academics in India

There are about 22 official languages in India and many local languages and dialects.

Generally students are taught in English and in one of their local official languages. In India, we have plenty of English speaking talent working in every field. So the language barrier to connect with the rest of the world is non-existent. As SAP Consultants our focus always is to provide the best-in-class services and experiences to the clients. And SAP Consulting firms encourage their employees to learn other international languages like German, Spanish, Japanese etc to work seamlessly.

Global Experience

I have majorly worked with clients located in North America, Canada, UK and Europe. And have travelled to the USA and the UK for work related assignments.

I got the opportunity to connect with people of different nationalities like Americans, Germans, Spanish, English, Czechs and Japanese during my stints in different SAP projects. And I have come to deeply value their principles like openness, transparency, honesty, hard work, discipline and perseverance.These values have not only shaped my character but have also become guiding lights, steering me through challenges and fostering personal growth.

 

Social Media 

I have been using LinkedIn as the medium to connect with other professionals and share or gain knowledge and perspectives. It provides the ability to stay connected with SAP Community members and influencers whom we have met during our professional journey and during various SAP Events. I have met Stephanie through LinkedIn and I’m looking forward to meeting her personally. 

I’m connected with SAP Mentors and SAP Champions on LinkedIn like @esjewett , @Raquel_Pereira_da_Cunha , @mohit_bansal3 , @ChandraBhushanSingh to name a few. I’m also connected with senior executives and senior SAP professionals through LinkedIn. I’m available on BlueSky SAP Community and would like to connect with other SAP Professionals there as well.

Personal Interests

My personal interests include reading, walking and listening to music. I’m an ardent reader and my reading list includes books on the topics of Management, Fiction and Technology. 

I believe in continuous learning and improvement . One of my favourite quotes is - “We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.” by Aristotle.

Walking helps me to stay active and fit. An evening stroll is also an opportunity to meet and chit chat with neighbours and hobnob with their pets. This, I believe, helps a lot in unwinding and de-stressing. I go to the gym two to three times a week. And I always look for a 5 minute break during work to stand up and walk a bit. I like listening to English and Hindi songs during my leisure time. 

I also like to go on vacation to explore a new place every year with my family. Within India, we have many options to choose from, you can opt for vacationing in mountains or beaches. Outside India, we have explored UAE, Malaysia and Vietnam. Here are some of the photos taken during my recent trip to Vietnam showing the famous Hanoi Train Street and Halong Bay.

Trends to watch out for

The ‘Next-Big-Thing’ continues to be AI. The GenAI trend will continue to evolve in different ways. From chatbots to Agentic AI, there are umpteen ways to apply GenAI usage patterns and bring value for the businesses. Unlike in the past when AI/ML used to be the domain of Data Scientists and Data Engineers, GenAI has turned the tables by providing tools which can also be understood and applied for business specific scenarios by the business users and analysts without the knowledge of Data Science or Machine Learning. Though a basic understanding of GenAI Concepts is still very much needed. 

Prompt Engineering and designing of Agentic AI Systems are very much in. In SAP or SAP BTP, GenAI appears in the form of SAP's Copilot called Joule. Joule comes with different flavours - Joule for Developers and Joule for Consultants. While Joule for Developers helps to free up developers time and increase their efficiency as well as helps in building quality code with human-in-the-loop approach. Joule for Consultants is intended to help consultants in SAP implementations and cloud transformations and helps to increase their productivity and speed up the implementations. Custom AI solutions in BTP will continue to have their importance for specific scenarios like recommendations and predictive analytics. Also Embedded AI brings instant value to the Business by just activating the relevant components in your SAP S/4 HANA instances.

Also talking about BDC which is a SAP’s fully managed SAAS solution for unifying and governing all the SAP data with third party data. Now businesses can leverage more contextual data than before to perform AI/ML operations and strategic analytics.This solution provides businesses with enhanced data products and robust data governance. And to top it all, SAP’s AI Ethics policies make sure that SAP delivers AI products and solutions with highest ethical, security, and privacy standards. Also, SAP’s cloud solutions play a key role in enabling a modern IT landscape which provides the flexibility to innovate and evolve faster with the dynamic market conditions.

With this I would like to conclude my blog. I would like to nominate @esjewett  who is a distinguished SAP Mentor and has contributed to the improvement of developers' experiences. I am confident he will share compelling experiences and perspectives that will enrich our discussions.

entry.title: API_SALES_ORDER_SRV pricing details with print flag
entry.content:
In ECC and on prem solutions, we can use RV_PRICE_PRINT_ITEM to assess which pricing conditions to display when accessing a sales order.

With the use of the API however, we just get back the entire list of price components and yet none of the subtotals come back.

We don't want to hard code logic to total up the JR1, JR2, JR3, JR4, JR5 tax conditions when we've already configured the pricing to subtotal these and a print flag of 'B' to show this at the header level, only if the value is not zero.

How can we do this when using the API?

entry.title: Create Custom fields to item layout in PR & PO in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
We have a S/4HANA public cloud and have a requirement to create a custom field in the item layout in PR and PO. Can you please suggest any approach?

We know how to create custom fields in item details panel. This is working fine but requirement is to create custom field at the line-item layout where we see all details like material, quantity, plant, delivery date etc.

entry.title: Re: Filter Variable on Fields with Compound Keys
entry.content:
I missed the description detail, sorry :(. With Account it is easy as the accounts tend to have the same description across chart of accounts. I had similar issue with cost elements / controlling area. Two possibilities

#concat key --> ugly in the front-end

#parameter for the upper level key in the Assoc. You pass the parameter from model/FACT-View to the Assoc. Consume the parameter in AM via variable (value passed from SAC). 

Disadvantage is with the parameters self as it will be mandatory. You also will need to decide if the user is to enter any value, or you do it in the background -> additional scripting. But this will work.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

 

We would need to set the Advanced Intercompany Sales (5D2) to support the scenario of selling a single material with multiple UoMs. 

My business requirement is that I want to create an intercompany order that can support multiple sales units (UoMs).  I still experience the error despite trying to follow the following note.

3298560 - FAQ new Advanced Intercompany Processes (IC-Sales and IC-Stock Transfer) Procurement.


Besides working with the aforementioned KBA,
I also try to achieve this by adding Sales Unit (VRKME) to the Condition Type PI01 by creating a new Condition Table / Access Sequence and map the Condition Type PI01 with the new Access Sequence.

However, the system does not allow us to create Intercompany PO as it has the error of unable to find the Purchase Price. (Noted that we have 2 UoMs in an order). 
If I change the Condition Type PIC1 on the Purchasing Side to accommodate Order Unit (BSTME), would that be possible to achieve this somehow? Is it a reversible process, if it does not work out and i would like to reset back to the standard settings?

Best Regards,

Weerakhan T.

entry.title: 🇺🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Palo Alto, CA, USA)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need any background in the topic, but you should have a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam, you will learn how to use the Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). This approach improves the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduces hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core, and how to query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Additionally, you will gain insights into key generative AI concepts and learn how to enhance RAG responses.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Wednesday, June 25rd in Palo Alto, CA from 09:00 to 16:00 US Pacific. The language of the event and the content will be in English.

The event is kindly hosted by Mark 9. Thank you!

Location:
SAP Labs, Inc. - Palo Alto
Building #1 - Pebble Beach
3410 Hillview Avenue
Palo Alto, CA 94304
USA

Please note that spaces are limited; we can only accommodate 30 attendees. Register here at the event page using the "Will you be attending?" functionality.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Microsoft Edge, Google Chrome etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: Configuring Product Vs Truck Incompatibility in SAP TM – Road Outbound Scenario
entry.content:
Business Scenario:

Product and truck type incompatibility in the supply chain refers to situations where certain products cannot be transported together or in specific types of trucks due to various constraints. This incompatibility can arise from factors such as safety regulations, product characteristics, and logistical requirements

Key Factors Leading to Incompatibility

  1. Safety Regulations: Certain products, especially hazardous materials, have strict regulations regarding their transportation. For example, flammable liquids cannot be transported with oxidizing agents due to the risk of explosion 
  2. Product Characteristics: Some products have unique characteristics that necessitate specific transportation conditions. For instance, perishable goods like fruits and vegetables require refrigerated trucks, while heavy machinery might need flatbed trucks.
  3. Logistical Requirements: Different products may have varying loading and unloading requirements. For example, some products might need to be loaded from the side, while others from the back, which can limit the types of trucks that can be used.

Importance of Managing Incompatibility

  1. Efficiency: Properly managing product and truck type incompatibility ensures that the right products are transported in the right trucks, optimizing the use of resources and reducing delays 
  2. Safety: Ensuring that incompatible products are not transported together minimizes the risk of accidents and ensures compliance with safety regulations 
  3. Cost Savings: By avoiding the use of inappropriate trucks, companies can reduce the risk of damage to products, which can lead to significant cost savings in terms of replacements and insurance claims 
  4. Customer Satisfaction: Efficient and safe transportation of products ensures timely deliveries and maintains product quality, leading to higher customer satisfaction 

SAP Solution

SAP TM provides a robust solution to handle product and truck type incompatibilities during transportation planning. By defining business rules that prevent certain products from being assigned to incompatible vehicle types, SAP TM ensures compliance with safety, regulatory, and logistical constraints. These incompatibilities are enforced at the Freight Order level, where Freight Units (FUs) carrying restricted products are checked against truck resources. The system uses condition-based logic to recognize and block invalid FU–truck pairings during both manual and automated planning. This ensures safe, efficient, and rule-compliant execution of road outbound logistics.

Step 1: Create Conditions for Product and Truck Incompatibility

In Fiori, go to “Create Condition” and define two separate conditions — one for products and one for trucks.

  1. Create the Product Condition:
    Define a condition that identifies the products that should be restricted from certain trucks.

     

  2. Create the Truck Condition:
    Define a second condition to identify the truck or vehicle type to which the product should not be assigned.     

     

Step 2: Create the Incompatibility Definition

In the “Create Incompatibility” app:

  • Incompatibility Area: Automatic planning and Manual planning
  • Incompatibility Type: Freight Unit – Vehicle Resource
  • Condition: Assign the one created in Step 1

This setup tells SAP TM that any Freight Unit carrying a product matching the condition should not be assigned to incompatible trucks.

Creating Product vs. Truck Incompatibility

 

Step 3: Create the Incompatibility Settings

Navigate to “Create Incompatibility Settings” Assign the incompatibility from Step 2

Defining Incompatibility Settings for Product vs. Resource

Step 4: Assign to Planning Profile

In the Planning Profile used for (Freight Order creation), assign this incompatibility settings:

  • Go to “Planning Profile”
  • Assign incompatibility settings.                

     

    Assigning Incompatibility Setting in Planning Profile

Step 5: Assign to Transportation cockpit

  • Go to cockpit Assign incompatibility settings.

     

Step 6: Testing the Incompatibility

Create a Sales Order in the ERP system containing the product that matches the Product Condition you configured earlier. Once the order is saved, SAP TM will automatically create Freight Units (FUs) based on the Freight Unit Building Rule.

Simulate Incompatibility

Go to the “Incompatibility” simulate function in SAP TM and test the following:

  • Select the created Freight Unit (with the restricted product)
  • Select a truck resource that matches the Truck Condition (i.e., the incompatible truck) ￼
  • Expected Result: The system will return an incompatibility message, indicating that the selected Freight Unit and truck cannot be planned together due to the active incompatibility settings. ￼

     

Evaluating Incompatibility in Both Manual and Optimized Planning

Manual Planning

In the Transportation Cockpit, when a Freight Unit (FU) containing a restricted product is manually assigned to an incompatible truck:

  • The system displays a warning message indicating the incompatibility.

     

     

Optimizer-Based Planning

  • Open the Transportation Cockpit.

  • Select the Freight Unit for automatic planning.

  • Run the optimizer

1. If only incompatible trucks are available:
The system does not create a Freight Order, as it respects the incompatibility rule.

2. If compatible trucks are available:
The optimizer bypasses the incompatible trucks and selects a valid resource, successfully generating the Freight Order.

Conclusion:

Addressing product and truck type incompatibility within the supply chain is crucial for optimizing efficiency, ensuring safety, and enhancing customer satisfaction. By leveraging SAP's advanced logistics solutions, businesses can effectively manage these incompatibilities, streamline their operations, and reduce costs.

This blog covered how to configure Product vs. Truck incompatibility in SAP TM to enforce safety and compliance during transport planning. In SAP, incompatibility can be assigned even for the manual planning scenarios and simulation of the incompatibility is a great tool to troubleshoot the results of incompatibility.

Embracing these technologies not only mitigates risks but also positions companies to meet the dynamic demands of the market with agility and precision. As the supply chain landscape continues to evolve, proactive management of transportation logistics will remain a key differentiator for successful enterprises.

entry.title: Bridging AWS IoT SiteWise and SAP S/4HANA: A Technical Deep Dive into Event-Driven Architecture
entry.content:
Introduction

In the era of Industry 4.0, the ability to respond to real-time events from industrial equipment is crucial for operational efficiency. This blog outlines a proof of concept (PoC) that demonstrates how to integrate events from AWS IoT SiteWise with SAP S/4HANA using SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and the Gen AI Hub. The solution leverages an event-driven architecture to automate business processes based on equipment anomalies detected in real-time.

This architecture is highly repeatable and can be applied not only for plant maintenance but also for broader SAP scenarios such as purchase requisitions, inventory updates, and more. Leveraging IoT SiteWise reference architecture, enterprises can scale these integrations seamlessly across different business processes.

This post focuses on a joint SAP + AWS industrial IoT scenario. Ganesh's blog on Power Utility Monitoring and Maintenance provides a very detailed perspective on the business problem and value proposition. In continuation, this blog dives deeper into the technical implementation of the PoC, providing step-by-step insights into how AWS IoT SiteWise integrates with SAP S/4HANA using SAP BTP and Gen AI Hub.

Engagement

The outcome described in this article was facilitated by an engagement between SAP and Amazon Web Services, which resulted in the creation and validation of a joint reference architecture use cases and PoC.  Teams from @PVNPavanKumar   (SAP Labs India) and Soulath Khan (Amazon Web Services) have participated in this engagement.

Business Scenario

Consider a smart factory where machines continuously stream telemetry data—vibration, pressure, and temperature—into AWS IoT SiteWise. A sudden spike in vibration could indicate early signs of mechanical failure.

Wouldn’t it be powerful if such events could automatically trigger downstream actions like

  • Creating a maintenance notification in SAP S/4HANA

  • Routing the issue to a workflow for triage and approval

  • Summarizing the incident using generative AI and notifying responsible personnel

This PoC showcases how we made that happen.

Technical Implementation

This architecture leverages key components across SAP BTP, AWS, and Gen AI Hub to create a scalable event-to-action pipeline. The Solution Architecture is illustrated in Figure 1, and the important components along with their connectivity are explained below:

  1. Event Source: AWS IoT SiteWise + Amazon S3

    • AWS IoT SiteWise collects real-time equipment data and sends processed events to Amazon S3.

    • A Lambda function monitors the bucket and forwards the relevant events to SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh.

  2. Event Transport: SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

    • The Lambda publishes events to SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (AEM), over an encrypted channel.

    • AEM is configured with topics and queues to ensure event durability and decoupled consumption.

  3. Event Processing: Events-to-Business-Actions Framework

    • This is the core of the application, built using Node.js and deployed on the SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Environment.

    • Responsibilities include parsing event payloads from AEM queues, triggering decision logic, and business APIs. It also enables business action configuration options, empowering users to specify downstream processes.

  4. SAP Build Process Automation (Decision Capability)

    • Event payloads are routed through SAP Build Process Automation, where decision logic is modeled to determine the corresponding business action that needs to be taken —e.g., create plant maintenance notification, material purchase requisition, log alert, or ignore.

  5. Gen AI Hub: Human-Readable Event Summary via Amazon Bedrock

    • The framework integrates with SAP AI Core to call a deployed Amazon Bedrock Claude 3 Sonnet model. 

    • This model takes raw, hard-to-read incoming IoT events and generates easy-to-read summaries of the event payload, which are then populated in the maintenance notification description for enhanced clarity.

  6. Business Action: SAP S/4HANA

    • As we have identified and processed the incoming event for Plant Maintenance Notification business action, the Events-to-Business-Actions CAP app then calls the S/4HANA Maintenance Notification API which creates the notification and returns the ID of the notification created. 

    • As both SAP BTP and SAP S/4HANA were on AWS , we have used the SAP Private Link Service and AWS Private Link Service to securely connect them. 

Developer Insights

As a developer building this PoC, a few key learnings stand out:

  • Loose coupling using AEM and queues ensures resiliency and scalability.

  • Gen AI Hub integration was seamless and added instant value for summarization use cases.

  • Decision capability in SAP Build allowed us to externalize complex logic from the app code, making it easier to maintain and extend.

  • Using SAP BTP Destinations and Service Bindings helped standardize secure backend access across environments.

Deployment Steps

Follow this GitHub documentation for detailed steps for configuration and deployment.

 

Closing Remarks

By combining event-driven architecture with Generative AI and SAP process automation, we’ve shown how enterprises can respond to physical-world events with business actions. We encourage you to explore the GitHub Repository, experiment with your own IoT events, and extend this foundation to fit your enterprise use case. 

Note: This blog is based on a proof of concept and is intended for educational purposes. For production implementations, additional considerations such as security, scalability, and error handling should be addressed.

On the SAP side, many thanks to Anbazhagan Uma from SAP for driving this topic by collaborating with all the stakeholders involved in the engagement; Praveen Kumar Padegal and Ajit Kumar Panda  for supporting the development of this solution, Madankumar Pichamuthu  for enabling collaboration, and finally Anirban Majumdar , PVN Pavan Kumar , Sivakumar N  for leadership support for this project.

On the Amazon AWS side, many thanks to Soulat Khan, Ganesh Suryanarayanan, Sunny Patwari, Renga Sridharan, Aiyappa Machanda, Abhik Ray, Suresh Pulivarthi, Krishnakumar Ramadoss and the SAP APN Partner Innovation team for their support in this project.

To learn more about SAP BTP, see the learning journey on SAP Learning called Discover SAP Business Technology Platform, a great introduction to BTP and the Intelligent Enterprise strategy, to see what it’s all about for free.

As part of the global partnership between SAP and AWS, we have jointly developed an SAP Learning Journey titled “Build Resilient Applications on SAP BTP with Amazon Web Services"

By joining this free openSAP course, you will delve into multiple Joint Reference Architecture patterns, gain in-depth knowledge of the architectural patterns, and have the opportunity to enhance your skills through hands-on exercises. 

To learn more about Amazon AWS, visit AWS training 

For more information about this topic or to ask a question, please contact us at paa_india@sap.com

entry.title: Re: Multiple datasources for custom Adobe Form
entry.content:
Hi EgbertVenema

I hope that someone who knows the exact answer can also reply as I am probably not the most qualified.

Looking deeper into the Runtime API (looks like for Developer Extensibility or BTP it is the same api)

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/3d8686d312bc426d8b2aa323473996b0.html?locale=en-US

or 

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/runtime-api-for-ads-rendering-calls?locale=en-US

The API only really cares about the XML data, the XDP form file, locale and specific options.

But you will need the XSD before you can properly define the form layout in Adobe Lifecycle Designer (it is needed to populate the Data View tab) - the Data View tab seems to be read only and doesn't seem to support edit to modify the XSD.

At some point, I guess you will need to do some manual work to combine the two data sources to define the data for your form.

I have not found that SAP makes a tool directly, but seems there are several XML to XSD convertors out there.

These two community posts seem related.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/graphical-xsd-builder-amp-xml-to-xsd-converter-a-step-forward/ba-p/13575519

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/convert-xml-to-xsd/qaq-p/654911

I hope that helps or someone else provides a better answer.

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: SAP Community Meetup Dortmund 11.06.2025
entry.content:
***English version below***

Glück auf,

am 11.06.2024 um 18:30 findet unser nächster SAP Stammtisch Dortmund, nun auch als SAP Community Meetup Dortmund bekannt, statt. Der Veranstaltungsort ist noch unbekannt, wird in der Dortmund Innenstadt sein. Die Lokalität wird zeitnah nachgereicht. 

Eingeladen ist jeder, der Lust und Zeit hat, unabhängig vom fachlichen Hintergrund, von Entwickler, Berater, Projekt Manager, Basis Administrator bis hin zum Endanwender.

Wir freuen uns über bekannte und neue Gesichter. Melde dich idealerweise bis zum 04.06.2025 an, damit wir passend planen können. Aber auch eine spontane Teilnahme ist gern gesehen.

Datum: 11.06.2024
Uhrzeit: 18:30
Wo: TBD

Bis dann!

----

Glück auf,

on June 11, 2025, at 6:30 PM, our next SAP Stammtisch Dortmund, now also known as SAP Community Meetup Dortmund, will take place. The venue is not yet decided but will be published soon.

Everyone is invited, regardless of their professional background, from developers, consultants, project managers, basis administrators to end users.

We look forward to seeing familiar and new faces. Ideally, please RSVP by the 4st so that we can plan accordingly. However, spontaneous participation is also welcome.

Date: June 11, 2025
Time: 6:30 PM
Where: TBD

See you then!
